rules.title: Display rating must have an accessible label
rules.description: A .rating element used as a display component must have role='img' and a non-empty aria-label that conveys the rating value (e.g. '3.5 out of 5 stars'). Without this, screen reader users receive no information about the rating.
rules.expected: element has role='img' AND non-empty aria-label
rules.message: Display rating is missing accessible label. Add role='img' and aria-label='X out of 5 stars' to the .rating element.
rules.fix: Add role="img" aria-label="3.5 out of 5 stars" to the root .rating element. Adjust the label to match the actual rating value.

rules.title: Star items must be hidden from assistive technology
rules.description: Individual .rating__star elements are visual representations of the rating value already communicated by the parent aria-label. Each star must carry aria-hidden='true' to prevent screen readers from iterating over them and announcing redundant information. All child SVGs must also carry aria-hidden='true' and focusable='false'.
rules.expected: element has aria-hidden='true' AND child SVGs have aria-hidden='true' focusable='false'

conditions: all descendant svg elements have aria-hidden='true'
all descendant svg elements have focusable='false'

rules.message: Star item .rating__star is visible to assistive technology. Add aria-hidden='true' to each .rating__star and aria-hidden='true' focusable='false' to child SVGs.
rules.fix: Add aria-hidden="true" to every .rating__star span. Add aria-hidden="true" focusable="false" to all SVG elements.

rules.title: Interactive rating must not suppress focus indicator
rules.description: When .rating--interactive is applied and .rating__star elements are keyboard focusable, the focus ring must be visible. outline:none on :focus-visible without a replacement violates WCAG 2.4.7.
rules.expected: computed styles include a visible outline or border — outline:none not present without replacement
rules.message: Interactive rating star has suppressed focus ring. Keyboard users cannot identify the focused star.
rules.fix: Remove any outline:none override on .rating--interactive .rating__star:focus-visible. Add a visible focus indicator via outline or box-shadow.

rules.title: Colors and sizes must reference design tokens
rules.description: All color and size values applied to .rating elements must reference CSS custom properties (var(--token-name)). Hardcoded hex, rgb(), px size values bypass the token system.
rules.expected: color, background, fill, width, height (when star-sized) all use var(--) — or are currentColor, transparent, none, inherit
rules.message: Hardcoded color '{value}' found on '{property}'. Replace with the corresponding --rating-* design token.
rules.fix: Replace e.g. color: #DBA102 with var(--rating-star-fill-default). See token surface in rating.contract.md §8.

rules.title: Interactive rating must use semantic form elements
rules.description: When the rating component is used as an input (user can select a rating), it must use a <fieldset> with <legend> and <input type='radio'> elements. Using div/span-based interaction without ARIA radiogroup pattern creates keyboard and screen reader failures.
rules.expected: element is a fieldset OR has role='radiogroup' with child role='radio' elements
rules.message: Interactive rating is not using a semantic form element. Use <fieldset> with radio inputs or add role='radiogroup'.
rules.fix: Replace <div class="rating rating--interactive"> with <fieldset class="rating rating--interactive"> and use radio inputs. See rating.contract.md §10.

rules.title: Rating must not use physical CSS properties that break RTL
rules.description: Physical directional properties on .rating or its children cause visual defects under dir='rtl'. The gap property is direction-agnostic. The half-star clip-path has a [dir='rtl'] override. Physical margin/padding must not be introduced.
rules.expected: padding-left, padding-right, margin-left, margin-right are absent on rating elements
rules.message: Physical CSS property '{property}' found on rating element. Replace with logical equivalent or use gap/flex for spacing.
rules.fix: Remove padding-left/right from rating elements. Use gap on the .rating container for star spacing.

rules.title: Default (gold) star fill color does not meet 1.4.11 non-text contrast
rules.description: The default gold color #DBA102 on white #FFF produces approximately 2.0:1 contrast. WCAG 1.4.11 requires 3:1 for non-text UI components. Brand green (#1B8354) passes at ~4.58:1. This rule flags usage of the default variant for review.
rules.expected: star fill color achieves at least 3:1 contrast against the component background
rules.message: Default (gold) star color #DBA102 on white = ~2.0:1 contrast. Does not meet WCAG 1.4.11 (3:1 required). Consider using the brand variant or raising the gold token.
rules.fix: Use .rating--brand for contexts requiring WCAG compliance, or raise the --rating-star-fill-default token to a darker gold value that meets 3:1 against the deployment background.

severity_legend.error: Blocks release. Must be resolved before component ships to production.
severity_legend.warning: Must be reviewed. Should be resolved; requires documented justification to waive.
severity_legend.info: Advisory. No action required but noted in audit report.

validation_status_legend.not_run: Rule has been defined but not yet executed against this component.
validation_status_legend.pass: Rule was executed and the component passed.
validation_status_legend.fail: Rule was executed and the component failed. See audit report for details.
validation_status_legend.skipped: Rule was intentionally skipped with documented justification.
validation_status_legend.manual_required: Rule cannot be validated automatically and requires human review.
